spec_match.rationale: The live preview fakes the alpha rendering using a bugged JS rgbaLiteral helper that results in near-zero opacities, and it fails to use the defined alphaColor helper that would actually test the native alpha() function.

interactive.rationale: Interactive color inputs update the generated tokens, CSS block, and the preview components in real-time.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The blurb matches the demo's purpose of generating a complete, alpha-based token set from single base colors.

edge_cases.rationale: Defines an alphaColor helper with a CSS.supports check for progressive enhancement but fails to use it in the actual preview rendering.

references.rationale: The parent index.html cites the ChromeStatus link, though it is missing from this concept page.

design_system.rationale: Adheres to design system layout, but the opacity calculation bug causes preview components to lose their background colors, violating visual design expectations.

openQuestions.title: Fix opacity calculation bug in live preview
openQuestions.detail: The JS passes decimals (like 0.08 for 8%) to rgbaLiteral which divides by 100, resulting in near-invisible rgba(r, g, b, 0.0008) backgrounds. Fix the values passed to be percentages (e.g. 8 instead of 0.08) or fix the helper.

openQuestions.title: Use native alpha() in live preview via alphaColor helper
openQuestions.detail: The code defines alphaColor which checks for native support, but uses rgbaLiteral instead. Switch the preview styling to use alphaColor so that native alpha() is used when supported.
